# W003 SHOULD fire for pointer access in "or" arguments — unlike "and",
# "or" returns the first truthy value, so later arguments can still be
# evaluated when earlier ones are nil/falsy.

check-templates -w
stderr 'W003'

-- go.mod --
module example.com/app

go 1.25.0
-- main.go --
package main

import (
	"embed"
	"fmt"
	"html/template"
	"io"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var source embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(source, "*"))

type User struct {
	Role string
}

type Page struct {
	Title string
	User  *User
}

func render(p Page) {
	_ = templates.ExecuteTemplate(io.Discard, "index.gohtml", p)
}

var _ = fmt.Sprint

-- index.gohtml --
{{if or .User .User.Role}}yes{{end}}
